name: Sericia — Post-deploy ISR warm-up (after Coolify deploys storefront)
_purpose: Every Coolify deploy restarts the storefront container, wiping the Next.js HTML cache and `unstable_cache` map. Without this workflow the first real visitor after a deploy pays a 2-6 second render bill. We listen for the Coolify deploy webhook and run `npm run warmup` which sequentially hits every URL in /sitemap.xml so the cache is hot before traffic lands.
_trigger: Configure Coolify → Storefront app → Webhooks → 'On deploy succeeded' to POST to this n8n webhook URL.

nodes.name: Coolify deploy succeeded
nodes.notes: Coolify fires this when the storefront deploy lands `finished`. Exposed at /webhook/sericia-storefront-deployed.

nodes.name: Wait 60s
nodes.notes: Give the new container time to boot, run payload migrations, and start serving — empirically 30-50s on CPX22; 60s is a comfortable margin.

nodes.name: Warm URL (each)
nodes.notes: Sequential mode (set executeOnce=false and concurrency=1 in the trigger settings). 200ms n8n inter-iteration delay = friendly pace; full sweep ~30s for 102 URLs.

nodes.name: Slack: warmup done
nodes.notes: Optional. Comment out if the daily cadence (every deploy = one ping) becomes noise.
